授業目的: このセミナーでは、世界経済における諸問題を紹介するとともに、データ・リテラシーやデータ分析における基本的な手法を教えます。セミナーの最終目標は、優秀な卒業論文を執筆するために必要な知識やスキルを身につけることです。
到達目標: 1. 卒業論文のテーマを決める。2. 卒業論文のために信頼性高いデータを収集し、ソフトウェア（Excel、STATAなど）を使ってデータを分析する。3. 悠主な卒業論文を執筆するために必要な知識やスキルを身につけ、インパクトのあるプレゼンテーションを行えるようになる。
授業方法: First, various issues in international economics and development will be introduced. Students will then learn to identify good sources of information, and they will have an opportunity to learn about data analysis through various exercises using spreadsheet software. Finally, students will formulate research questions for their graduation theses, and they will look for references and data which will help them  complete their theses successfully.

トピック.授業計画1:
The schedule of the seminar will be co-designed with students enabling progression throughout the year with an approximate schedule as follows:

April-May: Discussing various topics in international studies with a focus on economic development and the cross-border flows of people, goods and capital.

June-July: Coming up with good research questions for students’ graduation theses. For this purpose, students will be asked to find academic articles related to their specific topic of interest, and present the articles in class.

September-October: Identifying good sources of information, learning how to use references and how to structure a graduation thesis.

November-December: Learning how to find, visualize, analyze and interpret data and how to conduct different kinds of surveys. At the end of the year students will have to present the research methodology they have chosen based on their research questions.

Course Implementation Format："Face-to-face class"

Class hours: 30 classes x 90minutes =《2,700minutes》

参考文献・資料/Reference books0: Various reports by think tanks, newspaper articles, data sources and academic papers will be introduced throughout the seminar.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy and curriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Research Seminars:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.4. Ethical Values: Acquiring it though ‘liberal arts education for human growth’ based on Christian principles.
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note Evaluation is based on students' skills of finding relevant academic sources and data to be used in their theses, as well as on their skills of summarizing and sharing their research findings in academic settings. Evaluation criteria also include regular attendance, presentations and active participation in class.
